title: SessionAggregationDTO
description: Session-level rollup for a finalized optimization run (Traigent#1720/#1724 g2:agg-summary). Folded into POST /sessions/{id}/finalize as a top-level field and echoed back in the finalize response so the SDK can confirm the backend actually persisted it (never a pseudo-trial, never a local-only shim). CONTENT-FREE CONTRACT: every field is numeric, a metric-name label, a config identity (a config dict or a config HASH), or an anchored version token. Example inputs, prompts, expected outputs, and LLM responses are NEVER carried here — the producer builds this by an explicit allowlist, not by forwarding an internal summary object, and the server rebuilds it by the same allowlist, dropping any unknown key.

selection_mode.description: How the session winner was selected (e.g. aggregated_mean). A bounded label, never free text.

primary_objective.description: Name of the primary objective (a declared objective label, not content). Bounded label.

metrics.description: Sanitized session-level rollup metrics: metric-name label -> numeric value. Keys are bounded labels (propertyNames) so content cannot be smuggled into a key.

samples_per_config.description: Per-configuration sample counts, keyed by config HASH (not content). Keys are bounded labels (propertyNames).

best_weighted_config.description: The weighted-best configuration dict, or null. Config VALUES are already on the optimization wire — including, by the documented dataflow exception, prompt text a customer makes a tuned variable — so values are intentionally unconstrained here. Under SDK privacy mode this field is omitted (null) entirely, mirroring trial-config redaction; that is the control for customers who do not want config egress.

statistical_significance.description: Per-objective numeric statistical-significance badges; content-free. Keys are bounded objective/metric labels (propertyNames — content cannot be smuggled into a key); each value is a fixed badge object of integer trial-index lists, an anchored badge_name label, and an integer shared-example count. additionalProperties:false on the badge object rejects any nested content (prompts, outputs, LLM responses).

sdk_version.description: Anchored version token — never free text.
